steps.title: Identify what the question needs
steps.content: The task is to provide an example of a dinosaur fossil specimen's nickname. An example requires two things together: a specific nickname and the specimen it names.

steps.title: Eliminate the choices
steps.content:
• Choice A — A Pentaceratops fossil specimen from the Late Cretaceous period, which ended more than 65 million years ago, is housed at the New Mexico Museum of Natural History.: mentions the specimen but never states any nickname. ✗
  • Choice B — Dinosaur fossil specimens can be found at museums all over the world, and many of these specimens are given nicknames.: only a general claim; gives no specific nickname or specimen. ✗
  • Choice C — Nicknames are given to many dinosaur fossil specimens, including one housed at a museum in Albuquerque, New Mexico.: refers vaguely to a nickname without ever stating what it is. ✗
  • Choice D — Spike is the nickname of a Pentaceratops fossil specimen housed at the New Mexico Museum of Natural History in Albuquerque, New Mexico.: directly pairs the nickname Spike with the Pentaceratops specimen. ✓
That confirms D is correct.
